feedback_text:
DIAGNOSIS: 9 failing sub-attributes (55.9 pts recoverable), 0 passing. 2 regressions detected — recovery is top priority.

==================================================
REGRESSIONS (RECOVER THESE FIRST — highest priority):
==================================================

  REGRESSED: schema_correctness — was 75% at iter 2, now 61% (-14%)
    What was lost: The current version appears to be truncated mid-schema, cutting off at 'required: ["id",' in the tenant definition. The best version showed a complete schema with full definitions for plan, pricing_model with proper anyOf structures for nullable money references, and complete required arrays.
    Why it dropped: The schema content was cut off/truncated, making it incomplete. This affects schema_validity (incomplete structure), ref_usage (missing references to definitions that may be cut off), and constraint_quality (missing constraints from incomplete definitions).
    → RECOVER: Complete the truncated schema by restoring all missing content after the tenant definition's required array, ensuring all definitions (plan, pricing_model, usage_record, invoice, etc.) are fully present with their complete property sets, constraints, and $ref usage as shown in the best version.

  REGRESSED: schema_extensibility — was 75% at iter 2, now 68% (-8%)
    What was lost: The current version appears to be truncated and incomplete. The best version had a complete schema with full definitions for all entities (tenant, plan, pricing_model, etc.) and proper structure, while the current version cuts off mid-definition and appears to be missing substantial content after the tenant definition.
    Why it dropped: The current version is incomplete/truncated, showing only a partial tenant definition that ends abruptly with 'required: ["id",' without closing brackets or showing the rest of the schema. This makes it impossible to evaluate the full extensibility features that were present in the best version, even though the scorer's critique suggests the content quality is actually higher.
    → RECOVER: Complete the schema by ensuring all definitions from the best version are fully included and properly closed. The current version needs to show the complete JSON structure with all closing brackets, the full tenant definition, and all other entity definitions (plan, pricing_model, usage_record, invoice, etc.) that demonstrate the extensibility features the scorer is evaluating.

REQUIRED FIXES (ordered by point impact):

  FIX 1: schema_realworld.no_currency_handling (current: 0%, 8.0 pts at stake)
    Scorer's critique: ABSENT - Proper currency handling is present with ISO 4217 codes and cent-based amounts
    Measurement spec: Schema reflects real billing system patterns (Stripe-informed, not academic)
    Evidence found: "money": { "type": "object", "properties": { "amount": { "type": "integer", "description": "Amount in cents (smallest currency unit)", "minimum": 0 }, "currency": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^[A-Z]{3}$", "description": "ISO 4217 currency code" }
    ⚠ This is a PENALTY violation — removing it recovers points

  FIX 2: schema_realworld.calculated_total_without_line_items (current: 0%, 8.0 pts at stake)
    Scorer's critique: ABSENT - Invoice has line_items array structure
    Measurement spec: Schema reflects real billing system patterns (Stripe-informed, not academic)
    Evidence found: "line_items": { "type": "array", "items": { "$ref": "#/definitions/invoice_line_item" } }
    ⚠ This is a PENALTY violation — removing it recovers points

  FIX 3: schema_realworld.no_idempotency (current: 0%, 8.0 pts at stake)
    Scorer's critique: ABSENT - Usage records include idempotency_key field
    Measurement spec: Schema reflects real billing system patterns (Stripe-informed, not academic)
    Evidence found: "idempotency_key": { "type": "string", "maxLength": 255 }
    ⚠ This is a PENALTY violation — removing it recovers points

  FIX 4: schema_realworld.no_invoice_status_lifecycle (current: 0%, 8.0 pts at stake)
    Scorer's critique: ABSENT - Complete invoice status lifecycle is present
    Measurement spec: Schema reflects real billing system patterns (Stripe-informed, not academic)
    Evidence found: "status": { "type": "string", "enum": ["draft", "open", "paid", "uncollectible", "void"] }
    ⚠ This is a PENALTY violation — removing it recovers points

  FIX 5: schema_realworld.naive_date_handling (current: 0%, 8.0 pts at stake)
    Scorer's critique: ABSENT - Proper ISO date-time format is used consistently
    Measurement spec: Schema reflects real billing system patterns (Stripe-informed, not academic)
    Evidence found: "format": "date-time" appears throughout schema
    ⚠ This is a PENALTY violation — removing it recovers points

  FIX 6: schema_realworld.single_currency_assumption (current: 0%, 8.0 pts at stake)
    Scorer's critique: ABSENT - Multi-currency support is properly implemented
    Measurement spec: Schema reflects real billing system patterns (Stripe-informed, not academic)
    Evidence found: Multi-currency support with ISO currency codes in money definition
    ⚠ This is a PENALTY violation — removing it recovers points

  FIX 7: schema_correctness.schema_validity (current: 50%, 3.0 pts at stake)
    Scorer's critique: Schema is valid JSON Schema draft-07 with proper structure and type declarations. Implementation follows standard patterns correctly.
    Measurement spec: 1.0 if valid draft-07+, 0.5 if mostly valid, 0.0 if invalid
    Evidence found: Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute?: Standard JSON Schema implementation following rubric guidance without additional validation innovations
    ✗ FAILED CHECK: Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute?
      Evidence: Standard JSON Schema implementation following rubric guidance without additional validation innovations

  FIX 8: schema_correctness.ref_usage (current: 50%, 2.5 pts at stake)
    Scorer's critique: Good use of $ref for reusable definitions like money and metadata. Schema follows DRY principles appropriately.
    Measurement spec: 1.0 if DRY with $ref, 0.5 if some reuse, 0.0 if duplicated
    Evidence found: Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute?: Standard $ref usage following rubric guidance without advanced schema composition techniques
    ✗ FAILED CHECK: Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute?
      Evidence: Standard $ref usage following rubric guidance without advanced schema composition techniques

  FIX 9: schema_extensibility.metadata_support (current: 50%, 2.4 pts at stake)
    Scorer's critique: Good metadata support with flexible key-value structure applied to key entities. Implementation follows standard patterns correctly.
    Measurement spec: 1.0 if metadata object on key entities, 0.0 if closed
    Evidence found: Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute?: Standard metadata implementation following rubric guidance without additional extension mechanisms
    ✗ FAILED CHECK: Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute?
      Evidence: Standard metadata implementation following rubric guidance without additional extension mechanisms
